heading: Positional Operators
body:
You can use the positional operators to specify the position of the
terms that you want to search with the [term](span-term-ref)
operator. The positional operators are of type `document`. You must
specify at least one positional operator in your `span` operator
query. The positional operators can take other `span` positional
operators, recursively.

> **Note:** 
>
> About the Examples
>
> The examples on this page use the `sample_mflix.movies` collection.
> If you [Load Sample Data Into Atlas](sample-data)
> and [create a MongoDB Search index](ref-create-index) a [dynamic mappings](static-dynamic-mappings) index named `default` on the
> [Sample Mflix Dataset](sample-mflix) , you can run the following
> [$search](pipe.$search) sample queries against the collection. The sample
> queries use the [$limit](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/limit) stage to limit the results to
> `5` documents and the [$project](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/project) stage to exclude all
> fields except the `title` field in the results.

`span` takes the following optional positional operators.
